_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an average index of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice prices set at 100 in 2015, currently stands at 141, indicating a 41% increase over that period. This rise in the composite index is uneven across components and has developed relatively recently, reflecting shifts in select agricultural commodity prices rather than broad market trends. The index aggregates prices from global markets, offering a snapshot of these staple soft commodity costs over time.
notes.movers: The primary drivers of the basket's increase are coffee, which rose by 99%, and orange juice, up 34% since 2015. Wheat and sugar experienced more modest increases of 19% and 13%, respectively. These price moves largely reflect specific supply shocks linked to weather events like frost or drought impacting arabica coffee regions, and diseases such as citrus greening and hurricanes affecting orange juice production. These soft commodity markets are relatively thin, meaning even localized adverse conditions can cause pronounced price swings.
notes.why: The headline rise in the breakfast basket is mainly driven by a few weather-sensitive crops with inherently volatile supply conditions. Because of their exposure to climatic and biological risks, prices for these commodities can remain elevated over extended periods as recovery and replanting efforts take time. This underscores the importance of monitoring such factors in understanding soft commodity price dynamics.
